excluded_defs.name: stringify
excluded_defs.reason: read to confirm it calls replaceNewlines(convertToString(value)) — not modified

excluded_defs.name: convertToString
excluded_defs.reason: read to understand that arrays go through toJson() which produces JSON with \n escape sequences — not modified

exploration_log.aha_moment: Individual context values (via %context.key%) go through convertToString which returns the raw string — real newlines are then removed by str_replace. But whole-context arrays go through toJson() which encodes real newlines as JSON \n sequences (two chars), which str_replace misses. Extending str_replace to also handle these two-char sequences makes the escaping consistent.
exploration_log.hindsight: I spent time tracing exception normalization paths. The fix itself is a one-line change in replaceNewlines.

alternatives: Apply replaceNewlines to the final output string in format()
Override stringify for arrays to pre-escape values before JSON encoding
Extend replaceNewlines to also handle JSON \\n sequences
key_decisions.decision: Extend the str_replace in replaceNewlines to also replace literal \\n and \\r sequences
key_decisions.reasoning: This ensures consistent escaping whether context is accessed as a whole JSON object or as individual string values, without changing the control flow

search_sequence.result: Found that replaceNewlines only replaces real \n characters (not JSON escape sequences), and that arrays are JSON-encoded via convertToString -> toJson

search_sequence.action: Traced the path of context array vs individual context key through stringify()

search_sequence.result: Confirmed: %context% uses toJson on the whole array, producing JSON with \n sequences; %context.key% uses convertToString on the string value which keeps real \n that replaceNewlines then catches

queries.query_text: LineFormatter replaceNewlines does not escape JSON \n sequences in context arrays when allowInlineLineBreaks is false

solve_notes: Extended `replaceNewlines()` to also replace the two-character JSON escape sequences `\r\n`, `\r`, `\n` (literal backslash followed by n/r) in addition to real newline characters, so that context/extra data serialized as JSON has the same newline-free guarantee as individual string values accessed through `%context.key%` substitution.
task_text:
Fix LineFormatter not escaping newlines in context data

`LineFormatter` replaces newlines in the main message via
`allowInlineLineBreaks`, but context and extra arrays rendered by
`stringify()` can still contain raw newlines, breaking single-line log
parsing. Fix `LineFormatter.php` to apply newline escaping consistently
to all serialized parts of the output.

thrash_preventing_defs.name: stringify
thrash_preventing_defs.reason: read: an agent needs to understand that stringify calls replaceNewlines(convertToString(value)), and that arrays go through toJson() producing JSON with \\n sequences that the current replaceNewlines misses

thrash_preventing_defs.name: convertToString
thrash_preventing_defs.reason: read: confirms that non-scalar values (arrays/objects) are JSON-encoded via toJson(), while scalars return raw strings — needed to understand why JSON path has different newline representation

tier_difference_reasoning: The minimum set (replaceNewlines) is the edited method. An AI agent implementing this fix needs to understand WHY JSON-encoded arrays produce \\n sequences rather than real newlines, which requires reading stringify() and convertToString() to trace the path. Without those, an agent would not understand the asymmetry between scalar context values (real \n removed by str_replace) and array context values (JSON \\n sequences that the old str_replace missed).
